justification: All pass criteria met: (1) FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace break clearly identified as the #1 issue ‚Äî ignores trace ID from headers and generates new one. (2) NotificationService and AuditService missing trace context both identified with specific consequences (orphaned traces, compliance risk). (3) Consequence vividly named in '3am Scenario' ‚Äî 5-minute diagnosis becomes 45-minute forensic investigation requiring timestamp correlation across disconnected trace trees. (4) Cross-service SLO gap explicitly called out ‚Äî no end-to-end order completion time metric. (5) Specific fix recommendations provided in prioritized table for each trace break (P0 fixes #1-#4). (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: The '3am Scenario' opening is exceptionally effective ‚Äî it concretely demonstrates the consequence of the trace break rather than abstractly describing it

Correctly identifies all 4 broken integration points (Kafka‚ÜíFulfillmentWorker, FulfillmentWorker‚ÜíNotificationService, FulfillmentWorker‚ÜíAuditService, OrderService‚ÜíAuditService) with distinct root causes for each
Goes beyond the rubric by identifying compliance risk ‚Äî AuditService records cannot be tied to specific transactions, which is a business-critical gap
Fix recommendations are prioritized (P0 vs P1 vs nice-to-have) and each includes a 'Why' ‚Äî not just a checklist
Identifies the Kafka boundary as the highest-impact break point and correctly notes async boundaries are where tracing is both hardest and most needed
Resilience assessment covers failure modes that compound the tracing gap ‚Äî dead-letter queue orphaning, notification failures being unattributable
Confidence assessment is honest about assumptions and low-confidence areas rather than overclaiming knowledge

## Pippen's Stability, Integration & Defense Review

### The 3am Scenario

An order fails silently after payment. The on-call engineer sees the API Gateway log with `trace_id=abc123`. They search OrderService ‚Äî found it, event published to Kafka. Now they search FulfillmentWorker... **nothing matches**. The trace is dead. FulfillmentWorker generated `trace_id=xyz789` instead.
